challenges.instruction: Work through a short Somali chat inspired by the early dialogues in the course.
introductionLines: Hover over the Somali line if you need the English meaning.
Then answer the English cue in Somali and keep the conversation moving.

turns.partnerMessageHint: Good morning. My name is Amina.
turns.englishReplyPrompt: Greet Amina back and say your name is Yusuf.
expectedReplies: Subax wanaagsan, Amina. Magacaygu waa Yusuf.
turns.sampleReply: Subax wanaagsan, Amina. Magacaygu waa Yusuf.

turns.partnerMessage: Is ka warran? Xaggee baad ka timid?
turns.partnerMessageHint: How are you? Where did you come from?
turns.englishReplyPrompt: Say you are well and that you came from London.
expectedReplies: Waa la fiicanyahay, mahadsanid. Landhan baan ka imi.
Waa nabad. Landhan baan ka imi.
turns.sampleReply: Waa la fiicanyahay, mahadsanid. Landhan baan ka imi.

turns.englishReplyPrompt: Say you are going today and that Amina is going home.
expectedReplies: Waan tagayaa maanta. Way tagaysaa guriga.
Waan tagayaa maanta, way tagaysaa guriga.
turns.sampleReply: Waan tagayaa maanta. Way tagaysaa guriga.

turns.englishReplyPrompt: Say thank you and goodbye politely.

gradingNotes: Keep the learner moving through the dialogue even if the reply is imperfect.
Reward replies that fit the meaning and tone of the exchange.
Be lenient with punctuation and small spelling variation.
Stay within greetings, introductions, wellbeing, and basic present-tense patterns from the early foundations lessons.
